intro: Trigonopleura dubia, also known as 'Indian Pennywort', is a perennial plant native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, which are said to aid in reducing stress and improving cognitive function. The bioactive constituents of Trigonopleura dubia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
